claims.claim: false_interruption_timeout default is 2.0s and resume_false_interruption defaults True
_on_false_interruption: if can_pause and paused speech not done, calls audio_output.resume(), re-emits speaking state, emits AgentFalseInterruptionEvent(resumed=True)
claims.found: if ...['resume_false_interruption'] and (audio_output:=...audio) and audio_output.can_pause and not self._paused_speech.handle.done(): ...update_agent_state...; audio_output.resume(); resumed=True; ... emit('agent_false_interruption', AgentFalseInterruptionEvent(resumed=resumed))

claims.claim: On borderline interruption it pauses (not hard-cancel) current speech and arms a false_interruption_timer
claims.found: if speaking is False and self._paused_speech and (timeout:=...['false_interruption_timeout']) is not None: # schedule a resume timer ... self._start_false_interruption_timer(timeout)

claims.claim: If new speech was already scheduled, it drops the paused speech instead
claims.found: if self._paused_speech is None or (self._current_speech and self._current_speech is not self._paused_speech.handle): # already new speech is scheduled, do nothing; self._paused_speech = None; return

claims.claim: Deepgram lists 'Agent Responds Too Early / Premature Interruption' tied to aggressive end-of-turn thresholds, with no resume mechanism
claims.found: Agent Responds Too Early / Premature Interruption ... Usually originates in: Aggressive end-of-turn thresholds or speech boundary tuning. What to inspect: EOT threshold configuration (eot_threshold, eager_eot_threshold)... (no resume mechanism mentioned)

claims.claim: Requires audio output that supports pause/resume (can_pause) not just stop
claims.found: _pause_enabled: return bool(...['resume_false_interruption'] and ...['false_interruption_timeout'] is not None and self._session.output.audio and self._session.output.audio.can_pause)

summary: All code_refs and the deepgram source figure verified against ground truth. turn.py:117-125 confirms 2.0s timeout + resume default True. agent_activity.py:3669-3709 confirms the resume state machine (can_pause gate, audio_output.resume(), AgentFalseInterruptionEvent(resumed), drop-if-new-speech). agent_activity.py:1830-1866 confirms timer arming after end_of_speech. deepgram-ebook ~2051-2057 confirms the premature-interruption failure mode with no resume mechanism. No miscitations, no wrong claims.
